description_long: This is a double-ended open-end wrench made of metal, likely steel, with a slightly weathered, darkened finish. Each end features a U-shaped open jaw of different sizes, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The body is flat and elongated, with a slight curve at each end for better leverage and access in tight spaces. The surface shows some minor discoloration and patina, suggesting moderate use but no significant damage. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, and the handle is smooth without any added grip texture. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, suitable for mechanical or repair tasks in urban environments such as garages or workshops.
description: A double-ended open-end metal wrench with U-shaped jaws of different sizes and a slightly weathered, smooth finish. The tool is robust and utilitarian, showing minor signs of use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wrench, both open jaws clearly visible at each end.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting one open jaw and the flat, narrow profile of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the full length and both jaws from the reverse side.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, showing the other open jaw and the flat handle profile.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Minor discoloration and patina visible along the handle and jaws, but no significant rust or deep wear marks.
